tweet_text: hallow serves catholics. muslim pro serves islam broadly. youversion serves generic christianity. but methodists have their own prayers. orthodox christians follow a different liturgical calendar. baptists have unique traditions. identity-first communities deserve identity-first tools.

the long-tail of faith apps is completely unserved. 80M methodists. 100M baptists. 220M orthodox christians. 280M pentecostals. zero dedicated daily practice apps for any of them. the market isn't small. it's invisible to people who only build for broad categories.

streak mechanics work because they mirror spiritual discipline. you show up today. and tomorrow. and the day after. the number goes up by 1. not complicated. not gamified. just a quiet record of consistency. same principle as daily prayer.

a methodist doesn't need a catholic rosary tracker. a sunni muslim doesn't need a generic islamic app that mixes traditions. an orthodox christian follows a completely different liturgical calendar. specificity isn't a limitation. it's respect.

the communities already exist. r/OrthodoxChristianity (200K members). denomination-specific facebook groups with thousands of active members. church newsletters and bulletins. you don't need paid acquisition for faith apps. you need community trust. trust is the currency.

building tools for daily spiritual practice during ramadan. 25 days remaining. the urgency isn't marketing. it's real people wanting to track their fasting, prayers, and quran reading in one place. built for them, not for a pitch deck.
